content.problemStatement:
A shop has NNN items for sale. The iii-th item has a cost of AiA_iAi‚Äã.

You would like to purchase all NNN items.

The shop offers a discount: whenever an item is bought, all future purchases have their costs reduced by 111. However, the cost of an item also cannot fall below 000.

You can buy the items **in any order** you like. What's the minimum possible cost of purchasing all NNN items?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî the number of items in the shop.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum possible cost of buying all NNN items.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We buy the first item with a cost of 444. This then discounts the second item by 111, so we buy it for a cost of 222, with the total coming out to 4+2=64+2 = 64+2=6. This is optimal.
Test case 222: All the items have initial costs of 222. The process is as follows:

Buy one item with a cost of 222. This reduces the cost of everything else by 111, so they're all 111 now.
Buy one item with a cost of 111. This reduces the cost of the remaining two items by 111 again, so they both cost 000.
Buy one item with a cost of 000. This reduces the cost of the last item by 111 again. However, since it cannot go below 000, it remains 000.
Buy the last item with a cost of 000.

The total spent is 2+1+0+0=32+1+0+0=32+1+0+0=3.
Test case 333: Consider the following order:

Buy the second item, with a cost of 111. The other items' costs will reduce by 111.
Buy the fourth item, with a cost of 1‚àí1=01-1 = 01‚àí1=0. The other items' costs will reduce by 111.
Buy the first item, with a cost of 3‚àí2=13-2 = 13‚àí2=1. The other items' costs  will reduce by 111 again.
Finally, buy the third item with a cost of 4‚àí3=14-3 = 14‚àí3=1.

The total spent is 1+0+1+1=31+0+1+1 = 31+0+1+1=3.
